title: Premier league action of the past weekend
description: GEORGE NEWS - Pacaltsdorp United and Sussex...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Pacaltsdorp United and Sussex were both back on the winning side in the four matches on the Six Gun Grill SWD Premier League that were played last weekend.

Pacaltsdorp (70/3) won by 7 wickets after the defending champions dismissed Union Stars I for only 69. Enathi Kitshini (6/16) was in great form for Pacaltsdorp and as they surpassed the winning runs in only 13.1 overs, they also gain an important bonus point.


> Sussex, that had back-to-back trips to Oudtshoorn, also dismissed Union Stars II cheaply (100 all out) with Ravin-Lee Kemmies (3/45) the top performer with the ball for Sussex.


The D’Almeida-based team chased down the winning runs (103/4) in the 31st over for a 6-wicket bonus point victory.

Plett Pioneers (123 all out) remain winless and at the bottom of the log after they could not master the Knysna Cavaliers bowling attack of Francois Bonthuys (5/21) and Jarred Jardine (3/22). Cavaliers won the Bitou derby after they reached the target (124/4 in the 33rd over) for a 6-wicket bonus-point victory.

George (110/1 in 13.5 overs) were too strong for Harlequins (106 all out) and recorded a 9-wicket bonus-point victory. Werner Botha (49) and Cobus Snyman (45) excelled with the bat while Shelby Lindique (3/4) and Kelly Smuts (3/20) each bagged three wickets for George.


> Summarised score cards:


George beat Harlequins Forces by 9 wickets

Harlequins Forces 106 all out - 26.4 overs (Marco Jansen 33, Jermaine le Kay 30, Shelby Lindique 3/4, Kelly Smuts 3/20, Cobus Snyman 2/22)

George 110/1 - 13.5 overs (Werner Botha 49, Cobus Snyman 45)

Pacaltsdorp United beat Union Stars I by 7 wickets

Union Stars I 69 all out - 23.4 overs (Enathi Kiitshini 6/16, Grant Simpson 2/15)

Pacaltsdorp United 70/3 - 13.1 overs (Kurt Botha 25)

Knysna Cavaliers beat Plett Pioneers by 6 wickets

Plett Pioneers 123 all out - 35.4 overs (Tyrese Karelse 37, Francois Bonthuys 5/21, Jarred Jardine 3/22)

Knysna Cavaliers 124/4 - 32.1 overs (Keelan Gossayn 40, AB van der Walt 30*, Ruan Terblanche 25, Eden Tarentaal 2/26)

Sussex beat Union Stars II by 6 wickets

Union Stars II 100 all out - 29.3 overs (Ravin lee Kemmies 3/45, Rowan Groenewald 2/11, Geronovan Erasmus 2/12)

Sussex 103/4 (30.2 overs) (Johandré Barnard 41, Heath Richards 22*, Thomas Kaber 2/33)

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
